issues.title: Per-client tmux sessions so two clients on the same window aren't letterboxed
issues.body: aggressive-resize (shipped) makes each chat-tab window track its own current viewer, but two clients viewing the SAME task+window still share one tmux grid, so the larger terminal is letterboxed to the smaller. The only way to give same-window clients independent sizes is to stop sharing one session per task: give each attached client its own session in a session group (new-session -t <group>) and rework the switch-client task-switch model (direct.ts, tasks-pane/host.tsx, app.tsx, client.ts) to switch within the client's own session set. Larger refactor; ceiling is capped by tmux (grouped sessions still share window grids), so revisit only if same-window multi-client is a real use case. Deferred from the aggressive-resize fix; user chose the small A-tier fix first.

issues.title: Hover tooltip can flicker when the cursor moves onto it
issues.body: The v1 sidebar hover tooltip (cf1cd56) anchors just below-right of the cursor. Moving the cursor down into the tooltip's area leaves the hovered row → onMouseOut clears it → the row beneath re-pops it, so chasing it flickers. In a full-width tmux Tasks pane there's no room to the right, so below is the only anchor. Option to evaluate: a fixed detail panel pinned to the bottom of the sidebar (no cursor follow, zero flicker) instead of a floating tooltip.

issues.title: Hover tooltip is always-on — consider showing only when info was truncated
issues.body: The tooltip pops on every row hover, including on a wide pane where the branch + full title are already visible (only the worktree path is extra). Consider gating it to fire only when the responsive columns actually dropped the branch/changes or the title was ellipsised, so a wide pane stays quiet.

issues.title: Optional: CI check enforcing a changeset per PR
issues.body: Now that Changesets is adopted, add a CI step (changeset status gate, or the changesets/action bot) that fails a PR with user-facing code changes but no .changeset/*.md, so entries can't be forgotten. Tooling-only changes opt out with an empty changeset. Deferred from the Changesets migration to keep that change focused.
